decision_point: When surgical repair of a medium-to-large rotator cuff tear is indicated, is a single-row or double-row suture anchor technique preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: When surgical repair of a medium-to-large rotator cuff tear is indicated, is a single-row or double-row suture anchor technique preferred?
Options:
  1. Single-row suture anchor repair
  2. Double-row suture anchor repair
label: equivalent_options
label_rationale: Double-row repair provides superior biomechanical footprint coverage and lower re-tear rates, but single-row is faster, cheaper, and has equivalent clinical patient-reported outcomes.

reviews.notes: which-option label stratum via llm_haiku — Double-row repair shows superior biomechanical properties and lower re-tear rates on imaging, but single-row achieves equivalent patient-reported functional outcomes with lower cost and operative time, making this a famous debate where the clinical significance of biomechanical advantage remains genuinely uncertain.
